deterministic.engagement_rationale: 13 signals in the last 14 days from 9 contacts.

llm_produced.narrative: Engagement is high, with 13 signals from 9 contacts in the past 14 days, but sentiment is deeply negative — the account is in active escalation mode driven by widespread service disruptions and formal compensation demands. Between May 4 and May 6, multiple contacts reported outages: Reese White (May 6, 'Export functionality') flagged p99 latency still at 8x normal; Kendall Jones (May 6, 'Re: support ticket response' and 'Re: billing questions') reported 503 errors across endpoints despite a green status page; Morgan Jackson (May 5, 'Data retention policy') cited six hours of intermittent failures; and Morgan Anderson (May 4, 'Re: integration setup') raised data-loss concerns from a nightly batch job failure. Formal compensation requests came from both Kendall Johnson (May 6, 'Re: user provisioning') and Alex Wilson (May 5, 'User provisioning' and 'Re: compliance requirements'), and Drew Miller (May 6, 'Export functionality') formally requested an incident debrief. A compliance requirements API gap — a missing `account_status` field — was raised on May 1 with no documented resolution. On the positive side, Skylar Brown showed product activity on May 8 (integration connected, export started, workspace created), suggesting some continued build-out, though usage is limited to a single contact on a free plan.
